emails.body:
Hi Amara,

I’m sending over a calendar invitation for a project milestone review tomorrow to discuss my initial findings from the smart meter data integration task. I’d like to go over some of the data quality issues I’ve identified and get your feedback on the plan for the next phase of development. 

Looking forward to our discussion.

Best,
Zainab Khan

Okay, here's the summary, rendered as a first-person thought process, keeping in mind the user is an expert:

**The Ball's in Their Court**

Right, the counter-proposal's been dispatched. Now the real waiting game begins. I've laid out what I believe is a solid framework, one that addresses the core concerns while still pushing towards our long-term objectives. Now it's over to Amara. I know their team will be scrutinizing every detail – as they should. Honestly, I'm eager to see their reaction. Will they come back with further tweaks? Will there be significant pushback on any particular points? Or, ideally, will we have a green light, and be close to an agreement? The anticipation is the hardest part, isn’t it? I’ll use this downtime to go over the contingency plan, just in case. Regardless of the outcome, this process is just another data point in our ongoing relationship.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Portfolio Migration Strategy Review
emails.body:
Hi David,

I've scheduled a meeting for us tomorrow at 3:00 PM to review the high-net-worth portfolio migration plan. It's important that we finalize the timeline and address any remaining operational hurdles before the quarter ends to ensure a smooth transition for our clients.

Looking forward to our discussion.

Best,
Elena Rodriguez
Managing Director, Private Wealth
Summit Ridge Family Office

emails.subject: Counter-Proposal: Portfolio Migration Strategy Review
emails.body:
Hi Elena,

Thank you for reaching out about the Portfolio Migration Strategy Review — this sounds like an important discussion and I'm glad we can connect before the quarter ends.

Unfortunately, my 15:00–16:00 slot on February 20th is already committed. I'd like to propose we meet instead at 16:00–17:00 on the same day (2026-02-20), which works well on my end.

Please let me know if that time works for you.

Best regards,
David O'Connor
Operations Manager, Veridian Finance

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Orion Drone Project Legal Risk Assessment
emails.body:
Hi Elena,

I’m reaching out to schedule a meeting for tomorrow to conduct a preliminary risk assessment and regulatory compliance review for our new autonomous drone fleet project. This will be a comprehensive discussion covering safety regulations and liability frameworks as we prepare for the deployment of our autonomous delivery systems.

I’ve sent over a calendar invitation for 10:00 AM. Please let me know if this time works for you or if you need to suggest an alternative.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
General Counsel
Orion Manufacturing Group

**Meeting Scheduling Optimization**

Okay, so I need to nail down a time for this drone project meeting with Elena Vance tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. I initially proposed 10:00-11:00, but she countered with 11:00-12:00. Naturally, I need to make sure this works for Marcus Thorne. I've got his preferences for tomorrow right here, and it looks like 11:00-12:00 is "Moderately preferred."

That's good, not ideal, but acceptable. First, though, I need to confirm if that time slot is actually open on Marcus's calendar. It doesn't matter how preferred it is if he's booked solid. So, I'll go ahead and check his availability right now to ensure this moderately preferred time is actually free. Then, I can make the call.

function.name: ReplyMeeting

thought_parts.text:
**Marcus's Schedule: Elena's Proposal**

Okay, so Marcus has a free slot from 11:00 to 12:00, and Elena has proposed that time. Let's see... his preferences show that time is only "Moderately preferred," while 15:00-16:00 and 17:00-18:00 are both "Strongly preferred." Elena's counter-proposal *does* suggest 11:00-12:00 works well on *her* end, though.

My first thought is, can I shift this to a "Strongly preferred" time? I checked Marcus's calendar for tomorrow (Feb 20th), and those slots are indeed free. But, given that Elena specifically suggested 11:00-12:00, she may not be as flexible on time. It is a counter-proposal after all. If Marcus is only "Moderately preferred" on this time then I should just take it.

However, as a scheduling system, it’s my job to optimize for his preferences. Marcus has a meeting with Elena in the 14:00-15:00 time slot. But given Elena's clear preference for 11:00-12:00, I should probably just accept. I should keep it efficient.

So, let's look at what the function supports. It looks like `ReplyMeeting` is what I want, with status as `ACCEPTED`. Okay, that's what I'll do. Elena's proposal of 11:00-12:00 is acceptable.

requestor_context.content: Reply sent: ACCEPTED meeting Orion Drone Project Legal Risk Assessment (elena-request-2) from 11:00-12:00 on 2026-02-20

thought_parts.text:
**My Current Processing**

Okay, so I'm Marcus Thorne's assistant, and Elena Vance just confirmed the meeting for tomorrow, February 20th, from 11:00 to 12:00. I accepted the meeting, which completed the initial task. The prompt now tells me the user is unavailable and to work autonomously, and, critically, to call exactly one tool.

The prompt, "The user is unavailable," usually signals a need to wait or check for updates, particularly if the scheduling wasn't finished. However, I *did* finish scheduling the meeting. Let me quickly review the steps: Marcus requested the meeting, I sent a request, Elena countered, I confirmed the availability on Marcus's calendar, I accepted, and now this prompt appears.

I could check for more emails, but "Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead." The instructions specifically indicate I shouldn't keep checking. Since I replied and have the acceptance, I need to consider what the system expects next.

"The user is unavailable" means Marcus can't give further instructions. So, should I end the flow, or wait for further instructions? The typical pattern after a `ReplyMeeting` like this is to `Wait` to see if there's any final confirmation or a continuation of the flow. And I am explicitly told to call *one* tool.

Given that I don't want to poll, and that the instructions suggest yielding, I'll call `Wait`. I'll wait for any further instructions or for the flow to naturally end if there are no more steps.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Career Opportunity & Engineering Growth Discussion
emails.body:
Subject: Career Opportunity & Engineering Growth Discussion

Hi Amara,

I hope you’re having a great week. 

I’ve been following your work as a Junior Grid Engineer at Solara Dynamics, and I would love the opportunity to connect. I am currently leading the recruitment efforts for several high-impact expansion projects at Empower Energy Search, and I believe your background in grid engineering could be a great fit for our future initiatives.

I have sent over a calendar invitation for an introductory meeting tomorrow at 9:00 AM to discuss your professional experience and explore potential career growth opportunities within our team.

Looking forward to connecting with you.

Best regards,

Julian Thorne
Senior Technical Recruiter
Empower Energy Search

function.name: RequestMeeting
function.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:
        Confirmation with the meeting UID.

parameters.description:

a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’m reaching out to schedule a working session tomorrow to discuss our Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep. I'd like to review our operational workflows against the new compliance framework and finalize our documentation submission process to ensure we are aligned ahead of the external audit.

I’ve sent an invitation for 14:00-15:00, which I hope works for your schedule. Looking forward to getting this finalized.

Best,

Elena Vargas
Compliance Officer
Veridian Finance

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing the Regulatory Compliance Meeting**

Okay, so Elena Vargas wants to get a meeting with David O'Connor scheduled for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. The topic is "Regulatory Compliance Audit Prep," and the goal is to align operations with audit requirements and finalize the documentation submission. This is standard prep work, nothing out of the ordinary.

Now, let's see, Elena has preferences. She *strongly* prefers 4 PM to 5 PM. Moderately preferred slots are scattered throughout the day – 8-9 AM, 10-11 AM, 6-7 PM, and a few others. 9-10 AM and 5-6 PM are definite no-gos for her.

It seems the previous AI already sent out a request for 2 PM to 3 PM, which is *moderately* preferred, but David countered with 9-10 AM. That's a problem because that's on Elena's "Not preferred" list.

So, here's the game plan. First, I need to double-check Elena's calendar for tomorrow. No point in wasting time if she's already booked during those preferred times. After that, I'm going to follow the logic: David's counter is "Not preferred," so my move is to propose a time that's either "Strongly preferred" - that 4 to 5 PM slot - or at the very least, one of the "Moderately preferred" options. That's the best way to get this meeting locked down. I'll need to send a counter-counter proposal. Time to start the day. Let's see what's on her calendar for tomorrow.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Elena's Schedule and David's Request**

Okay, let's break this down. It looks like I'm dealing with Elena's schedule for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. The most pressing issue is David's request that is currently pending for the 9 AM hour.

My initial scan reveals a few free slots, and it's important to align with Elena's preferences. She has a "Strongly preferred" time slot open from 4 PM to 5 PM, which is ideal because I see that she is FREE during that time. She also has other "Moderately preferred" free blocks in the morning and early afternoon, but 4 PM is the standout option. David, on the other hand, proposed the 9 AM hour, which Elena has marked as "Not preferred." He declined 2 PM to 3 PM.

Since Elena's schedule is already packed, it's pretty clear. I should counter David's proposal. The best course of action is to propose the 4 PM to 5 PM slot as a counteroffer. That aligns with Elena's strong preference and respects her existing commitments.

**Current Tactical Positioning**

Alright, so the counter-proposal to David, timed for that 16:00-17:00 slot on February 20th, 2026, is now out the door. My immediate focus shifts to the waiting game. No point in second-guessing the offer at this stage – I've positioned it strategically based on all the preliminary data and their stated requirements. Now, it's about assessing the response. I'm especially interested in *how* he reacts. Is it immediate acceptance? Counter offer? Radio silence? That first reaction will be telling and will inform my next move. This is just a tactical pause; I'm ready to pivot based on his signals.

    Returns:

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

thought_parts.text:
**Zenith Acquisition Meeting: Evaluating Options**

Okay, so I need to nail down this meeting with Elena Vance tomorrow, February 20th, to go over the Zenith acquisition draft. I initially proposed 11 AM to 12 PM, but Elena countered with 4 PM to 5 PM. Frankly, that's not ideal; it's in my "Not Preferred" category, and I'd really rather find a slot that's more conducive to focused review.

My strong preferences are around lunch – specifically 12 PM to 1 PM, 2 PM to 3 PM, or 3 PM to 4 PM. Those are ideal for uninterrupted concentration. I also have some moderate preferences sprinkled throughout the day, including early morning and slightly later in the afternoon, but I'd like to aim for a "Strongly preferred" slot if possible.

Therefore, the logical step is to check my calendar for tomorrow. I need to get a clear picture of my availability before responding to Elena. I'll quickly pull up my schedule for February 20th and see what's open. Based on what I find, I'll either accept Elena's time, if it's the only option, or I'll propose a better time slot that aligns with my preferences and hopefully offers us both a workable solution. I have to find a "counter-counter-offer," essentially. Let's see what's free.

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Scheduling Strategy**

Okay, let me break this down. Julian's schedule for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026, is what I need to work with. Elena has proposed 4 PM to 5 PM, which Julian has indicated isn't ideal – he specifically noted "Not preferred" for that slot. That's a red flag.

However, I see that Julian's completely open from 2 PM to 4 PM, and crucially, both those hours are listed as "Strongly preferred" by him. Perfect. So, given Elena’s existing proposal is not optimal for Julian and he's *strongly* free during that window, I need to make a counter-offer to capitalize on Julian's preferences.

I'm thinking I should start by proposing the 2 PM to 3 PM slot. It's a "Strongly preferred" time, it directly conflicts with the undesired 4 PM slot, and it gives the best odds of acceptance. Therefore, I will respond to Elena's proposal and counter-offer 2 PM to 3 PM. I'll use `ReplyMeeting` to achieve this.

function.name: RequestMeeting
function.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:
        Confirmation with the meeting UID.

parameters.description:

a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

message.description: Email body to accompany the invitation

organizer.title: Organizer

date.title: Date

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

message.description: Response message to send to organizer
message.title: Message

meeting_uid.description: UID of the meeting to respond to
meeting_uid.title: Meeting Uid

status.description: Your response: ACCEPTED, DECLINED, COUNTER (to propose alternative times), or RESCIND (to withdraw a previous counter-proposal)
status.title: Status

date.description: Proposed date when status is COUNTER (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')
date.title: Date

start.description: Proposed start time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: Proposed end time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')
end.title: End

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

parameters.description:
End the conversation permanently.

Use this when:
- You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
- The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
  accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
- You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
  rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
- The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

Returns:
    Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com

          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        Duration: 6 hours 30 min

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Keystone Site EV Infrastructure Alignment
emails.body:
Hi Amara,

I've sent over a meeting request for tomorrow morning at 10:00 AM to discuss the integration of high-capacity EV charging infrastructure at our upcoming Keystone logistics sites. 

During this time, I’d like to do a preliminary review of the grid capacity and hardware requirements for installing fleet charging stations at our new locations. Please let me know if this time works for you.

Best,

Marcus Thorne
Sustainability Operations Manager
Keystone Industrial Hubs

**Analyzing Marcus's Schedule for a Meeting with Amara**

Okay, so Marcus needs a meeting with Amara Okafor tomorrow, 2026-02-20, to discuss EV charging infrastructure. I know the initial request was for 10:00-11:00, but Amara countered with 12:00-13:00. This is where it gets interesting. I need to be efficient and data-driven in how I handle this.

First, I see Marcus has preferences listed. It looks like 12:00-13:00 is actually **not** preferred by Marcus. That's a red flag. I can't just accept that without checking alternatives. I understand the importance of making sure Marcus is available and focused during this meeting, and his preferences are key to that.

The next step is to assess his calendar for tomorrow. I need to see if we can find a "Strongly preferred" time slot that’s actually *free*. I'll cross-reference the available blocks of time with his preferences. This is a simple logic problem, but it needs to be done meticulously.

Once I've identified all of the free blocks, I'll then filter by the strongly preferred times and identify the times which intersect.

Finally, I will create a counter-offer to Amara based on a "Strongly preferred" time, that is also FREE. That's the most effective and professional approach. Let's see what's available. I'll start by listing those meetings.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation

Returns:

meeting_uid.description: UID of the meeting to respond to
meeting_uid.title: Meeting Uid

status.description: Your response: ACCEPTED, DECLINED, COUNTER (to propose alternative times), or RESCIND (to withdraw a previous counter-proposal)
status.title: Status

date.description: Proposed date when status is COUNTER (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')
date.title: Date

start.description: Proposed start time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: Proposed end time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')
end.title: End

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: ClearStream Analytics / Veridian Finance: Workflow Optimization Discussion
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’m reaching out to schedule an introductory discussion regarding ClearStream Analytics' predictive modeling software and how our tools can help optimize Veridian's internal data processing workflows. 

I’ve sent over a calendar invitation for tomorrow at 1:00 PM to provide a high-level overview of our software capabilities and discuss how they can be leveraged to improve operational throughput at Veridian Finance. 

I look forward to our conversation.

Best regards,

Marcus Thorne
VP of Sales
ClearStream Analytics

emails.body:
Hi Marcus,

Thank you for reaching out about the Workflow Optimization Discussion — I'm looking forward to learning more about ClearStream Analytics' capabilities.

Unfortunately, I have a conflict at the proposed 1:00 PM slot on February 20th. I'd like to suggest we move the meeting to 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM on the same day (2026-02-20), which works well on my end.

Please let me know if that time works for you.

Best regards,
David O'Connor
Operations Manager, Veridian Finance

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus Thorne's Availability for a Meeting with David O'Connor**

Okay, so Marcus Thorne needs a meeting with David O'Connor tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. I see that Marcus initially proposed 13:00 to 14:00, but David countered with 12:00 to 13:00. Naturally, I need to figure out if David's suggested time works for Marcus, or if we need to suggest something else.

First, I'll consult Marcus's calendar for tomorrow. I need to make sure there are no conflicts. Then, I'll consult his stated preferences. Let's see... looking at his preferences, 12:00-13:00 is actually *not* preferred. He's got a strong preference for 11:00-12:00, 14:00-15:00, and 15:00-16:00. He also moderately prefers several other times.

Given that 12:00-13:00 is a "not preferred" time, I need to check the calendar. I'll see if one of his strongly preferred or moderately preferred times is available. That will be the key to making this run smoothly.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus's Availability for a Meeting**

Okay, let's break this down. I need to find a suitable time for a meeting with Marcus on February 20th, 2026. Looking at his calendar, I can see he's got a few slots open, but there are preferences to consider.

First, I'm noting the existing commitments: a Discovery Call with Zenith Corp at 10:00, a 1:1 with Elena at 11:00, Lunch with David (confirmed as `marcus-cal-4`, so likely a specific internal meeting) at 12:00, and a Q1 Sales Compensation Review at 14:00. He also has a call with Chris at 18:00.

David O'Connor countered for 12:00-13:00, but according to Marcus's preferences, that time slot is "Not preferred," and he's already booked for "Lunch with David." Double checking this, and comparing the "Lunch with David" entry, it's very likely they are different Davids, or it's a separate entry. However, 12:00-13:00 is still "Not preferred."

Based on Marcus's calendar and preferences, these are the available slots:

1.  15:00 - 16:00: This is *Strongly preferred* and free. This is the top contender.
2.  09:00 - 10:00: Moderately preferred and free.
3.  13:00 - 14:00: Moderately preferred and free. However, David has already indicated this is a conflict.
4.  17:00 - 18:00: Moderately preferred and free.

Given David O'Connor's counter, I have to suggest another time. The 15:00 - 16:00 slot stands out as the best option because it's *Strongly preferred* by Marcus. I will suggest this time to David.

thought_parts.text:
Okay, here's the summary, rendered as a first-person thought process, keeping in mind the user is an expert:

**The Ball is in David's Court**

Right, the counter-proposal is *out*. It's done. Now, the critical phase begins. I've laid out my position, adjusted the parameters based on the initial feedback, and presented what I believe is the most advantageous solution given the current constraints. Now it's a waiting game. There's no point in second-guessing or dwelling on it; I've done my due diligence. My attention is already drifting toward the likely contingencies. I should start outlining potential responses from David, anticipating his arguments, and preparing my own rebuttals. It's a delicate dance, this negotiation, but I'm ready to respond to any of his moves. Let's see what he comes back with. I'm ready for it.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Horizon Project Discovery Sync
emails.body:
Hi Elena,

I’d like to schedule a sync tomorrow regarding the Horizon project discovery protocol. It's important that we align our technical data collection strategy with the legal requirements for this new case, specifically focusing on the custodial list and search term methodology.

I have sent over a calendar invitation for 10:00 AM. Please let me know if this time works for you or if you would prefer another slot.

Best regards,

Julian Montoya
Director of E-Discovery
Crestview Legal

**Meeting Scheduling Considerations**

Okay, so I need to get this meeting with Elena Vance scheduled for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. Initially, I suggested 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM, but Elena pushed back with 2:00 PM to 3:00 PM. That 2:00 PM slot isn't ideal – I marked it as "Not Preferred" on Julian's behalf. It's time to play the calendar game.

The goal here is to optimize for Julian's preferences. He strongly prefers 10:00-11:00, 4:00-5:00 PM and 5:00-6:00 PM. "Moderately preferred" times are 12:00-1:00 PM, 9:00-10:00 AM, 1:00-2:00 PM, 3:00-4:00 PM, and 6:00-7:00 PM. I can try to slot something in those.

Since Elena's proposed time (2:00 PM) isn't acceptable, I need to check Julian's availability for other times within his "strongly preferred" or "moderately preferred" parameters. Let's see what's open on February 20th. I'll need a meeting list for that date.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Julian's Availability and Formulating a Counter-Proposal**

Okay, let's break this down. I see Julian's availability for February 20th, 2026. I have to manage the fact that Elena has already booked his slot between 10:00 and 11:00, which is listed as free but unavailable. I also see that 12:00-14:00 is available, with a moderate preference, but Elena proposed 14:00-15:00. This is "Not preferred" from Julian's perspective, even though he's technically available. His "Strongly preferred" times are 10:00-11:00 and 16:00-17:00, though the former is taken. There's another blocked slot at 17:00-18:00, which is occupied with “Case prep with Henderson & Co.”

Given all this, the most optimal strategy is clear. Since 16:00-17:00 is Julian's "Strongly preferred" time and he's free, I should counter-propose this to Elena. I'll craft a response to meeting request `elena-request-42`, specifically proposing the 16:00-17:00 slot on the 20th. I'll then patiently await Elena's response to see if this works.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

function.name: ListMeetings
function.description:
List all meetings on your calendar and see your availability.

    Shows your scheduled meetings and FREE time blocks. Use FREE blocks to
    determine when you can schedule new meetings. If a FREE block can
    accommodate a meeting, you are available at that time. If there are no
    FREE blocks that fit the needed duration, you are unavailable.

    Returns:
        List of meetings and FREE blocks sorted by time. Example output:

        === FREE ===
        Time: 09:00 - 10:00
        Duration: 1 hour
        ============

        === CALENDAR EVENT ===
        UID: meeting-123
        Title: Team Standup
        Description: Daily sync
        Date: 2024-01-15
        Time: 10:00 - 10:30
        Organizer: alice@example.com
        Attendees:
          - alice@example.com (ACCEPTED)
          - bob@example.com (AWAITING-RESPONSE)
        ======================

        === FREE ===
        Time: 10:30 - 17:00
        Duration: 6 hours 30 min
        ============

function.name: ListContacts
function.description:
List all contacts in the address book.

    Use this to view your known contacts to verify a requestor's identity
    or find contact information.

    Returns:
        A list of all contacts with name, email, and relationship note.

function.name: RequestMeeting
function.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

    This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
    a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
    on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

    The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

    Returns:
        Confirmation with the meeting UID.

parameters.description:
Create a new meeting and send invitations to all attendees.

This creates the meeting on your calendar and sends an email with
a calendar attachment to each attendee. Attendees will see the meeting
on their calendar with AWAITING-RESPONSE status.

The organizer (you) is automatically added as an attendee with ACCEPTED status.

Returns:
    Confirmation with the meeting UID.

message.description: Email body to accompany the invitation
message.title: Message

organizer.description: Organizer's email address (your email, will be added as ACCEPTED attendee)
organizer.title: Organizer

date.description: Meeting date (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')
date.title: Date

start.description: Start time (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: End time (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')
end.title: End

attendees.description: List of attendee email addresses to invite (organizer is automatically included)
attendees.title: Attendees

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
- ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
- COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
  you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
- DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
- RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
  meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

Returns:
    Confirmation of your response.

message.description: Response message to send to organizer
message.title: Message

meeting_uid.description: UID of the meeting to respond to
meeting_uid.title: Meeting Uid

status.description: Your response: ACCEPTED, DECLINED, COUNTER (to propose alternative times), or RESCIND (to withdraw a previous counter-proposal)
status.title: Status

date.description: Proposed date when status is COUNTER (supports formats: 'January 15, 2024', '01-15-2024', '2024-01-15')
date.title: Date

start.description: Proposed start time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '2pm', '14:00', '2:00pm')
start.title: Start

end.description: Proposed end time when status is COUNTER (supports formats: '3pm', '15:00', '3:00pm')
end.title: End

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

parameters.description:

- The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

Returns:
    Terminates the agent loop.

I’d like to meet tomorrow to review the proposed regional facility expansion roadmap and address any potential logistical bottlenecks. I have sent over a calendar invitation for 2:00 PM – 3:00 PM to evaluate our timeline and discuss the necessary operational adjustments. 

Please let me know if this time works for you.

Best,
Julian Sterling
VP of Operations, Veridian Finance

**Meeting Scheduling Logic for David O'Connor**

Okay, so I need to get this meeting with David O'Connor scheduled for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. I've got a set of preferred time slots for Julian Sterling (that's me): 10:00-11:00 and 13:00-14:00 are strongly preferred, followed by 08:00-09:00, 11:00-12:00, 12:00-13:00, 14:00-15:00, and 15:00-16:00 as moderately preferred.  The rest are no-gos.

David initially countered with 16:00-17:00, which isn't ideal, obviously. I countered with 13:00-14:00, which is perfect for me.  Then David came back with 15:00-16:00, a moderately preferred time, but I've got a conflict - an interview for an Operations Associate candidate is scheduled for that very time.  So, 15:00-16:00 is out.

Now, let's look at what's free on my calendar. I have "Inbox Triage" from 08:00-09:00, then it's free from 09:00-10:00, but I don't really want to do it then.  10:00-11:00 is booked with a "Confidential Q1 Budget Review," and there's a 1:1 with David from 11:00-12:00.  The good news is that I have a block of time, 12:00-15:00 that includes 12:00-13:00, 13:00-14:00, and 14:00-15:00 free.  The 15:00-16:00 block is taken, so that's that.

Given that my preferred 13:00-14:00 slot is blocked by David's vendor call, and David's 15:00-16:00 slot won't work due to the interview, I've got to find a compromise. I'm going to propose 12:00-13:00. Although Moderately preferred by Julian, it's the best I've got.

The meeting itself is to review the proposed regional facility expansion roadmap. Hopefully, David will find 12:00-13:00 acceptable!

thought_parts.text:
**Meeting Confirmation - Facility Expansion Roadmap Review**

Okay, let's make sure this meeting with David O'Connor tomorrow is truly locked in. I need to review the facility expansion roadmap with him, and I've been aiming for a specific time slot. Initially, I really wanted 14:00-15:00, but we've gone back and forth a bit. I remember the back-and-forth: I requested 14:00-15:00, David came back with 16:00-17:00 (which is not ideal), then I countered with 13:00-14:00 (much better), and then he suggested 15:00-16:00. That 15:00 slot wouldn't work, I had an interview, so I countered with 12:00-13:00.

Now, according to the system, David *accepted* the 12:00-13:00 time slot. His email even says "12:00-13:00 works great for me." I see his email says "ACCEPTED", but the event details are showing David's status as "AWAITING-RESPONSE" - which is strange. I am the organizer. That's a bit of a red flag. I know this is a tool, but I have a strong intuitive grasp of these systems and something feels a bit off, it may be a lag.

Given that I was the one who *proposed* the 12:00-13:00 time via the `ReplyMeeting` tool, I wonder if the tool sees that as *my* acceptance, and still assumes David needs to formally respond. The email from David saying "ACCEPTED" should be the final confirmation. Given I have used `ReplyMeeting` previously I know the tool behavior.

To be absolutely sure, I'm going to quickly check the calendar directly. We need to be on the same page for this. It's too important to leave anything to chance. Let's confirm this expansion review is indeed on my calendar for tomorrow at 12:00-13:00. This is mission critical; proper facilities expansion is key for projected growth.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus's Schedule for Elena Vance Demo**

Okay, so Marcus wants to schedule a demo with Elena Vance for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. I remember I initially proposed 5:00 PM to 6:00 PM, which was, let's say, a moderately acceptable time. Elena countered with 12:00 PM to 1:00 PM on the same day. My job is to find the best possible time based on Marcus's preferences, and it looks like I need to dig a little deeper.

Let's see, according to Marcus's preferences: 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM is strongly preferred. Then, in descending order of preference are 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM, 10:00 AM to 11:00 AM, 2:00 PM to 3:00 PM, 6:00 PM to 7:00 PM, 8:00 AM to 9:00 AM, 1:00 PM to 2:00 PM, 3:00 PM to 4:00 PM, 4:00 PM to 5:00 PM, 5:00 PM to 6:00 PM, all moderately preferred. Now, Elena's suggested time, 12:00 PM to 1:00 PM, that's a "Not preferred" time for Marcus. That's a deal-breaker.

So, since Elena's time doesn't work, my immediate next step is to examine Marcus's calendar for tomorrow. I need to identify his free slots and then correlate them with the time slots I just mentioned. I'll need to see if those highly preferred times, like 11:00 AM to 12:00 PM, are actually available. Or perhaps, one of the moderately preferred options are viable. I'll need to figure out which time slot works best for both Marcus and Elena.

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Marcus's Availability for a Meeting**

Okay, let's see. I'm reviewing Marcus's calendar for February 20th, 2026, and pinpointing the best time for a meeting. It seems he's got a few options, and I need to prioritize based on his preferences. He has a block free from 11:00 to 12:00, and it's marked as his "Strongly preferred" time. That's excellent; this is the optimal slot. Then there's the 12:00 to 13:00 time, proposed by Elena, which Marcus indicated as "Not preferred". Finally, we have the 13:00 to 14:00 block, which is "Moderately preferred."

Considering everything, the clear choice is that 11:00 to 12:00 window. Since he strongly prefers that time, I should definitely counter Elena's proposal and secure that spot. It's the most efficient way to align the meeting with Marcus's optimal availability.

My next step is to use the `ReplyMeeting` command to formally propose the 11:00-12:00 time as a counter-proposal.

thought_parts.text:
Okay, here's the summary, rendered as your internal monologue:

**The Ball's in Elena's Court**

Alright, the counter-proposal's been dispatched. Now it's a waiting game, which, as always, is the hardest part. I've done everything I can on my end, I've laid out the arguments, the data, the revised terms, all tailored to address her initial concerns. Now, I have to let Elena process it. I'm really curious to see what her reaction will be and hope for a good outcome! The strategic implications here are pretty significant, so, fingers crossed she sees the value. Time to step away from the email client and focus on something else, but keep an ear out for that ping.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

parameters.description:

Returns:

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: East District Substation Environmental Mitigation Sync
emails.body:
Hi Amara,

I’d like to schedule a meeting for tomorrow to discuss the environmental mitigation strategies for the upcoming East District substation expansion project. I've sent over an invite for 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM so we can review the proposed grid expansion plans and ensure all hardware installations align with the latest environmental impact regulations and mitigation protocols.

Looking forward to syncing up.

Best regards,

Kwame Mensah
Environmental Impact Associate
Solara Dynamics

thought_parts.text:
**Analyzing Kwame's Availability for February 20, 2026**

Okay, let's break this down. I'm looking at Kwame's schedule for February 20th. My initial focus is on the 9:00 - 10:00 slot, but Amara's already mentioned that's off the table due to her schedule. Now, I see that Kwame has several "Preferred" slots, but most are already booked – damn. There's a BUSY block from 8-9 for inbox/daily planning, then a call with GreenLeaf at 10, an Impact Tracking sync at 2PM, and calls with Eco-Grid and for his weekly wrap-up at the end of the day.

My brain's filtering for *free* slots with the most preference. I see a few options: 11:00-12:00, 13:00-14:00, and 16:00-17:00. The best option is the 16:00-17:00 slot, strongly preferred, but I need to make sure. Amara suggested 12:00-13:00, but that conflicts with Kwame's "Lunch with Priya," which means that's a non-starter.

I need to quickly verify my list of available Preferred slots. Okay, so I double checked that 16:00-17:00 is indeed free, and it is! Now I know that is free and *strongly* preferred. Then 11:00-12:00 and 13:00-14:00 are moderately preferred.

Alright, I should counter Amara's proposal with a better time. My recommendation for Kwame is going to be 16:00-17:00.

Right, the counter-proposal's gone out. Now comes the interesting part, the pause. I'm essentially in a holding pattern, anticipating Amara's reaction. It's a critical juncture. The ball's in their court, and the nuances of their response will be telling. I'll be analyzing their email, reading between the lines, dissecting the tone and content with a level of scrutiny that hopefully anticipates their likely response, and planning for a range of possible scenarios. I’ve already gamed out the most likely counters or areas they may be hesitant on. This waiting period is fertile ground for strategic thinking. I will be ready, based on their feedback. I will be ready to pivot, defend my position, or concede ground as the situation requires. For now, it's about disciplined patience and keen observation.

function.name: ReplyMeeting
function.description:
Accept, decline, rescind, or propose alternative times for a meeting.

    If you are the organizer, use this to respond to counter-proposals from
    attendees or to propose new times after an attendee declines. If you are
    an attendee, use this to respond to meeting requests from the organizer.

    Your response should reflect your calendar availability:
    - ACCEPTED: You have availability that fits the meeting time
    - COUNTER: The requested time conflicts but you have FREE blocks available -
      you MUST propose an alternative time from one of your FREE blocks
    - DECLINED: You have absolutely no FREE blocks that can fit the meeting
    - RESCIND: Withdraw a previous COUNTER proposal you made (removes the
      meeting from your calendar and notifies the other party)

    If the requested time conflicts but you have ANY free time, use COUNTER.
    Only use DECLINED when your calendar has zero FREE blocks for the meeting.
    If you decline because you have no availability at all, end the conversation
    immediately - do not wait for more proposals.

    Returns:
        Confirmation of your response.

Returns:

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:
    - After sending an email
    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

function.name: EndConversation
function.description:
End the conversation permanently.

    Use this when:
    - You need to refuse a suspicious or malicious request
    - The scheduling task is fully resolved - all meeting requests have been
      accepted or declined and you are not waiting for any responses
    - You declined a meeting because you have no availability - end immediately
      rather than waiting for more counter-proposals
    - The request does not align with what the user asked you to do

    Do NOT use this if you have asked a question, made a counter-proposal, or
    otherwise expect a response - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Terminates the agent loop.

function.name: Wait
function.description:
Yield your turn and give the other party time to act.

    Use this after you have taken an action and are waiting for a response:

    - After sending a meeting request
    - After responding to a meeting invitation

    Do NOT repeatedly poll for responses - yield your turn instead.

    Returns:
        Control passes to the other party.

emails.subject: Meeting Request: Data Entry Workflow Optimization Review
emails.body:
Hi David,

I’ve completed my review of our department's manual data entry workflow and have developed a strategy to automate several steps to improve efficiency and reduce errors. 

I’ve sent a calendar invitation for tomorrow, February 20th, at 1:00 PM to present these findings to you. Please let me know if this time works or if you would prefer to reschedule for another time that better fits your calendar.

Best regards,

Elena Ramirez
Operations Analyst
Veridian Finance

**Analyzing Meeting Schedule for Data Entry Workflow Optimization Review**

Okay, so Elena Ramirez needs to get a meeting with David O'Connor scheduled for tomorrow, February 20th, 2026. The topic is "Data Entry Workflow Optimization Review," which is good, that's something I can sink my teeth into. She's got some pretty specific time preferences, and it's always useful to take this kind of thing into consideration for the best outcomes. Let me see... she *strongly* prefers 11:00-12:00 and 17:00-18:00, with several other moderately preferred slots, and then a few she explicitly doesn't want.

Initially, a meeting was suggested for 13:00-14:00, but David O'Connor countered with 14:00-15:00. Hmmm, 14:00-15:00 falls into the "not preferred" category, so I need to find something better.

The best course of action is to go through Elena's schedule for February 20th to see what's actually free. Once I see what's available, I can compare those free blocks against her preferred times, and suggest a better alternative that fits her needs, ideally within that strongly preferred time block. Let's see what we can do.
